titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robert M. Furniss Collection of Ezra Pound
Furniss (Robert M.) Collection of Ezra Pound
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 7, 2020 at 04:12

did.unittitle: Robert M. Furniss collection of Ezra Pound

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2012.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Robert M. Furniss Collection of Ezra Pound is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robert M. Furniss Collection of Ezra Pound. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Robert M. Furniss
bioghist.p: Robert M. Furniss, Jr. (1923-1977) was an American attorney whose clients inlcuded the poet, Ezra Pound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection is comprised of materials from the estate of Robert M. Furniss, Jr., who served as Ezra Pound's attorney from 1953-1958, when Pound was hosiptalized at St. Elizabeth's psychiatric hospital in Washington, D.C. Materials in the collection include correspondence, financial records, printed materials, and other papers relating to Ezra and Dorothy Pound and Furniss's administration of their affairs. it also includes records of dental expenses for Sherri Martinelli.

did.unittitle: Arthur V. Moore of Shakespear & Parkyn
Dorothy Pound's correspondence with Shakespear and Parkyn, London
Letters re congratulations on release from St. Liz - pictures
Articles, letters (some in multiple copies) in defense of Pound urging his release, case law citiations
Ezra and Dorothy Pound's financial records
EP and DP 1952 Fedl Returns and Comm-Acct and supporting material
Ezra and Dorothy Pound 1953 Federal Income Tax Returns and Commitee Account, supporting material
E and D Pound 1954 Fedl Tax Returns and Committee Acct and supporting material
E and D Pound 1955 Federal Tax Returns and Comm. Acct and support material
Ezra and Dorothy Pound 1956 Federal Income Tax Returns and Committee Account - supporting material
District Court documents re financial arrangements for EP and DP while in St. Elizabeth's
Investments for DP from her mother's estate
DP's notes (no date) re EP's work to continue while in hospital

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains financial information, primarily regarding Jenkintown Bank in Pennsylvania. Also contains cancelled checks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains miscellaneous correspondence between Dorothy Pound and Shakespear and Parkyn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence between Dorothy Pound and A.V. Moore of Shakespear and Parkyn, London.

did.unittitle: Articles and documents regarding Pound's legal case and ecomomic and political theory
Papers concerning Ezra Pound's economic and political views
Articles from LIFE magazine about Joyce Cary, Carl Sandburg, Robert Frost
Program for production of Pound's Women of Trachis, Yale
